channel.title: Index on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in Index on mahmood

item.title: inner product
item.description: an inner product on a vector space is an operation on pairs of vectors in that satisfies the same conditions that the dot product in euclidean space does: namely, bilinearity, symmetry, and positive definiteness. a vector space equipped with an inner product is an inner product space. (Joseph A. Gallian, 2021 chapter 1 inner product spaces) if , we define the inner product to be the quantity (terence tao, 2023 definition 5.

item.title: set
item.description: we define a set to be any unordered collection of objects, e.g., is a set. if is an object, we say that is an element of or if lies in the collection; otherwise we say that . for instance, but . (terence tao, 2022 chapter 3 set theory) let be a set. for any object , and any object , suppose we have a statement pertaining to and , such that for each there is at most one for which is true.

item.title: open ball
item.description: open ball for any and any , the open ball of radius around is the subset (JOHN H. HUBBARD, BARBARA BURKE HUBBARD, 2015 definition 1.5.1) note that must be less than for the ball to be open; it cannot be . in dimension 1, a ball is an interval.

item.title: A* algorithm
item.description: A* (pronounced like “a-star”) is a best-first search algorithm that uses the evaluation function where is the path cost from the initial state to node (referring to the path we took to get to which would be the estimated shortest path from the initial state to ), and (heuristic function) is the estimated cost of the shortest path from to a goal state, so we have represents the past knowledge acquired while searching.

item.title: logic formulas
item.description: note that and represent true and false, respectively propositional logic equivalence number equivalence rule name E1 E2 E3 E4 E5 E6 E7 E8 E9 E10 E11 E12 E13 E14 distributivity E15 distributivity E16 de morgan E17 de morgan E18 E19 E20 E21 E22 E23 logical implication number implication rule name I1 I2 I3 I4 I5 I6 I7 I8 I9 I10 I11 I12 I13 I14 first-order logic logical implication number implication I'1 I'2 I'3 I'4 I'5 I'6 I'7 I'8 I'9 I'10 rule of inference name rule of inference Universal Instantiation where is some arbitrary element of the universe Universal Generalization where holds for every element of the universe Existential Instantiation where is some element of the universe of discourse.

item.title: common lisp
item.description: for loop (loop for x in '(1 2 3) collect x) ;; returns list (loop for x in '(1 2 3) do (print x)) ;; prints the numbers functions (defun <name> (list of arguments) "docstring" (body)) plist defining a plist (setf my-plist '(alt "yes" bar "no")) retrieve a value by key (getf my-plist 'alt) change the value that corresponds to a key (setf (getf my-plist 'alt) 45) get a list of the attributes (loop for (key value) on my-plist by #'cddr collect key) unbind variable (makunbound 'foo) undefine funciton (fmakunbound 'function) quicklisp need to install it manually afaik curl -O https://beta.

item.title: probability distribution
item.description: the probability distribution is a function that gives the probabilities of different outcomes for an experiment. if is a discrete random variable then the list of values that it could receive and their corresponding probabilities are called the distribution law, probability law, or the distribution of said variable let be a random variable. the probability measure is called the distribution of . for a real random variable , the map is called the distribution function of (or, more accurately, of ).

item.title: 2d rotation about the origin
item.description: a rotation of a point about the origin through an angle maps it to another point such that p and and p’ are at the same distance from the origin and the angle from the vector p to the vector p’ is to determine the coordinates of the image point , it is very convenient to use polar coordinates. let , where is the distance from to the origin and the polar angle.
